opp.name: IC S2P
opp.next_step: February 20, 2026: Dear Caroline, Thank you very much for the detailed follow-up and for sharing the materials and next steps. We appreciate the comprehensive summary and the access to the Showpad content and meeting recording — it will be very helpful for our internal reviews. lead from EY
opp.ais_why:
• Product scope includes ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake and ML iAnalyze (four AI SKUs out of a broader S2P stack including eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iRisk, iSource, iSupplier) ‚Äî signalling Zycus has positioned the deal with AI as part of the value proposition. But no Description text is captured in SFDC and no Avoma meetings are tied to the opp ID for transcript-based AI sentiment.
• Zero Avoma meetings on file, null SF Description, no LastActivityDate, BD_Engagement is NaN in SFDC. There is no demonstrated buyer engagement on AI capabilities to quote ‚Äî the deal exists in SFDC at Qualified stage but the captured rep-buyer dialogue is essentially nonexistent. SFDC data quality is the primary constraint here.
• Low confidence: relying entirely on product-list metadata. The four AI SKUs in scope keep this above pure AI Resistant but the complete absence of any buyer dialogue, demo, or champion identification means we cannot ground a higher score. Default DOWN at 4/5 boundary held; would move UP only with call evidence of AI conversation or DOWN to 3 if the opp remains dormant.

sources: Avoma demo call 2026-02-09, EY framing: 'iContract is currently exploring opportunities to digitize its procurement processes and significantly reduce manual workloads'
Buyer questions on SAP budget integration, external budget file workaround, stock-check during request definition (2026-02-09 transcript)
metrics.narrative: IC Hotels articulated a clear qualitative pain on the 2026-02-09 demo call: the need to digitalize procurement processes and significantly reduce manual workloads (per EY's Arda Karacelebi framing the session). Buyers asked operationally grounded questions about SAP integration (budget sync, purchase order flows, stock position checks during request definition), external budget file uploads as a workaround if SAP budgeting is not yet active, and warehouse stock requests bypassing PO creation, indicating real process friction around ERP integration, budget control, and manual requisition-to-fulfillment steps. However, no quantified business case was presented: no headcount savings target, cycle-time reduction goal, cost-per-transaction baseline, error-rate metric, or ROI figure. The demo was technical fit validation, not value quantification. Given the 137-day silence since, no subsequent value workshop or ROI discussion has occurred. The next move is to restart engagement with a focused ROI scoping session tied to IC Hotels' ERP modernization timeline and quantify the manual-workload reduction IC expects, anchored to their multi-property procurement volume.

sources: Avoma demo call 2026-02-09, sole IC Hotels buyer: Ceyhun Akdemir (CIO)
Zero follow-up engagement, zero introductions, zero advocacy behaviors post-demo
137 days of silence; Next Step 2026-02-20 was a polite acknowledgment, not a commitment
Salesforce: 0 OpportunityContactRoles, single-thread exposure
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. The sole IC Hotels participant on the 2026-02-09 demo was Ceyhun Akdemir, Chief Information Officer. A CIO can be a sponsor or influencer on a procurement-digitization play, but there is zero evidence of champion behaviors: no follow-up call, no internal advocacy, no introduction to the Economic Buyer or procurement leadership, no push to advance the evaluation, no competitive intelligence shared, and no response to the materials sent on 2026-02-20 (per the Next Step acknowledgment). The relationship went silent immediately after the demo, 137 days ago. Ceyhun has not re-engaged, has not introduced Zycus to other stakeholders, and has not driven next steps. EY (the partner) is more active than the buyer, which is a red flag: EY recommended Zycus to IC Hotels, but if EY cannot compel IC Hotels to move, EY is a sponsor without pull. The opportunity has zero contact roles attached, meaning no multi-threading has occurred beyond Ceyhun. The next move is to re-engage Ceyhun via EY (the last point of contact), assess his current priority and timeline, and ask for an introduction to the procurement or operations leader who would own the business case and budget for this transformation. Without a champion inside IC Hotels who has urgency, access to the Economic Buyer, and willingness to advocate, this deal will remain stalled.

sources: Salesforce: Competitors__c null
Avoma demo call 2026-02-09: no competitor mentioned
EY stated 'recommended Zycus as a strong fit' but did not name alternatives
competition.narrative: No competitor has been named or identified in any source. The Competitors__c field in Salesforce is empty. The 2026-02-09 demo call contains no reference to other vendors, no mention of an RFP shortlist, no competitive questions ('how do you compare to X'), and no indication that IC Hotels is running a bake-off. Arda Karacelebi (EY) stated that 'our team has evaluated potential solutions and recommended Zycus as a strong fit,' which could imply EY ran an internal vendor scan and shortlisted Zycus, but the other vendors EY considered (if any) were not disclosed. The absence of named competition could mean (a) EY sole-sourced Zycus based on prior relationship, (b) IC Hotels has not yet formalized a competitive process and is in early discovery, or (c) a competitor exists but was not discussed on this call. Given the deal is in Qualified stage (pre-formal evaluation) and has been silent for 137 days, it is most likely that no structured competitive process has started. The risk is that when IC Hotels or EY does launch a formal evaluation, a competitor will emerge that we are unaware of. The next move is to ask EY directly whether IC Hotels is evaluating other procurement-suite vendors (SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, Ivalua, GEP, or regional/hospitality-focused platforms) and what EY's recommendation rationale was (why Zycus over alternatives).

sources: Avoma demo call 2026-02-09, EY Arda Karacelebi: 'iContract is currently exploring opportunities to digitize its procurement processes and significantly reduce manual workloads'
Buyer questions 2026-02-09: SAP integration, external budget file upload, stock-check workflows, PO-bypass for warehouse requests
identify_pain.narrative: IC Hotels' pain is clear and operationally grounded: manual, fragmented procurement processes across a multi-property hospitality portfolio, with heavy reliance on manual workload and lack of digitalization. Arda Karacelebi (EY) articulated it explicitly on the 2026-02-09 demo: 'iContract is currently exploring opportunities to digitize its procurement processes and significantly reduce manual workloads.' The buyer questions during the demo reveal the specific friction points: SAP ERP integration gaps (budget data sync, PO creation workflows), workarounds for incomplete ERP coverage (needing to upload budget files externally if SAP budgeting is not yet active), stock-position visibility during requisition (not post-hoc), and the need to streamline or bypass PO creation for internal warehouse requests. These are classic symptoms of a partially digitalized, ERP-adjacent procurement function where requisitioners and approvers are doing manual reconciliation, spreadsheet-based budget tracking, and duplicate data entry. The pain owner is Ceyhun Akdemir (CIO), though procurement-process pain typically also sits with a CPO or operations lead (not yet identified). The pain is confirmed; what is missing is the quantified cost of that pain (hours per requisition, error rates, compliance risk, delayed spend visibility) and the compelling event that would drive urgency to solve it now. The 137-day silence suggests no burning platform has emerged.

sources: No contracting discussion on 2026-02-09 demo call
EY partner involvement suggests possible mediated or bundled contract structure, unconfirmed
Salesforce: Current_Contract_Expiration__c null, no partner co-sell terms documented
paper_process.narrative: Paper process and contracting mechanics are entirely unknown. No discussion of MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec requirements, procurement channel (direct vs. via EY as implementation partner), or contract structure occurred on the 2026-02-09 demo call. The call was technical discovery, not commercial negotiation. Given EY's heavy involvement, it is likely that if the deal advances, contracting will be mediated or bundled through EY's consulting engagement (EY as prime, Zycus as subcontractor, or a co-sell arrangement), but that structure has not been confirmed. No Current Contract Expiration date is populated in Salesforce, so there is no incumbent-renewal forcing function. No partner agreement or co-sell terms with EY are documented in the opportunity. The next step is to clarify with EY whether Zycus contracts directly with IC Hotels or through EY, and to map IC Hotels' legal/InfoSec/procurement approval requirements early (hospitality enterprises often have multi-property legal review and centralized InfoSec gates).

sources: Avoma demo call 2026-02-09 attendees: ceyhunakdemir@ichotels.com.tr (CIO, technical participant)
Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: 0 buyer-side roles attached
Next Step 2026-02-20 references 'lead from EY' but does not name the IC Hotels budget authority
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. The sole buyer participant on the 2026-02-09 demo was Ceyhun Akdemir, Chief Information Officer at IC Hotels. A CIO on a procurement-digitization play is typically a technical sponsor or influencer, not the commercial authority (that role usually sits with a CPO, CFO, or COO in a hospitality/multi-property enterprise). No pricing, commercial terms, budget approval process, or ROI discussion occurred on the call or in any subsequent activity. The Next Step from 2026-02-20 references 'lead from EY', suggesting EY may be mediating or co-selling, which further obscures who holds the purse strings on the IC Hotels side. The account-level contact list includes Ümit Sunay (Central Purchasing Manager, Antalya Airport) and Tolga Yeşildal (another CIO title), but neither has been engaged in this opportunity's scope. With 137 days of silence, the Economic Buyer remains unmapped, unengaged, and unconfirmed. The critical next move is to ask EY or Ceyhun Akdemir directly who controls the procurement-transformation budget and approval authority at IC Hotels (likely a CPO or CFO), then secure an intro.

sources: Avoma demo call 2026-02-09, EY-led session with 6 EY consultants and 1 IC Hotels buyer
No approval chain, timeline, or process documented in call or Salesforce fields
137-day silence suggests process stalled or paused
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown. The single buyer touchpoint (2026-02-09 demo) involved IC Hotels' CIO plus six EY consultants (Basak Kuyucu, Arda Karacelebi, Berk Ilhan, Kutay Almak, Deniz Ozmen listed on the call), suggesting EY is driving or co-managing the vendor selection on behalf of IC Hotels, a common pattern in large procurement-transformation engagements where the client delegates discovery to the implementation partner. However, no approval chain, decision timeline, committee structure, or evaluation stages were articulated. Arda Karacelebi's framing ('our team has evaluated potential solutions and recommended Zycus') implies EY has already shortlisted Zycus informally, but whether IC Hotels' leadership has endorsed that recommendation, what governance IC Hotels requires (CFO sign-off, board approval, multi-stakeholder committee), or what the next gate is (RFP, POC, commercial proposal, EY project charter approval) remains undocumented. The 137-day silence since the demo suggests the process is either paused, waiting on IC Hotels' ERP roadmap, or stalled within EY's internal project pipeline. The next move is to ask EY directly (via the named EY leads on the call) what the decision process and timeline are on IC Hotels' side, who the decision-maker is, and what triggers advancement to the next stage.

sources: Avoma demo call 2026-02-09, buyer questions on SAP integration, budget/stock checks, PO-bypass flows
EY framing 2026-02-09: 'walk us through the tool and present the live demo that will help us better understand how their capabilities fit for iC tourism'
No RFI/RFP/BRD shared; stage Qualified (pre-formal evaluation)
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are emerging but not formally documented. The 2026-02-09 demo questions reveal IC Hotels' implicit evaluation lens: seamless SAP ERP integration (budget sync, PO creation, stock position visibility), flexibility to handle partial ERP readiness (external budget file upload as a bridge), requisition-to-warehouse flow efficiency (skipping PO creation for internal stock requests), and the ability to validate stock and budget availability during request definition (not post-submission). Arda Karacelebi (EY) framed the session as assessing whether Zycus is 'a strong fit for iC tourism, not only in terms of current capabilities, but also as a platform that can shape a solid feature proof process vision,' indicating criteria around future-proof architecture and current vs. roadmap fit. However, no formal RFI, RFP, or written BRD has been shared. No scoring rubric, no weighted criteria list, no comparison framework against other vendors. The deal is pre-Shortlisted, so structured criteria likely have not been formalized yet. The next step is to request IC Hotels' business requirements document or procurement-transformation mandate (if one exists) and co-author a formal evaluation framework with EY and the buyer, anchored to the integration and manual-workload reduction priorities surfaced on the demo.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No direct AI appetite signal from the buyer. AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are all null in Salesforce, so no AI excitement has been captured or scored. The demo call title includes 'Agentic AI Powered Procurement Solutions,' but the buyer questions were purely operational (ERP integration, budget sync, PO flows), with no questions about AI, Merlin, or agentic capabilities in the transcript excerpt. EY's framing ('a platform that can shape a solid feature proof process vision') hints at future-proof/innovation interest, but that is consultant language, not buyer-stated AI curiosity. Without follow-up engagement, it is unclear whether IC Hotels sees AI as a differentiator or is focused solely on traditional procurement digitalization. Tier: AI Curious (inferred from the demo title and future-vision framing, but not confirmed by buyer behavior or questions).

items.detail: Deal has been silent for 137 days with no buyer engagement since 2026-02-09 demo. Close date 2027-02-16 (235 days out) is a placeholder with no credible path from Qualified stage to close. If IC Hotels has deprioritized or shelved the procurement transformation, the timeline is irrelevant.

EY is heavily involved (6 EY attendees on demo vs. 1 IC Hotels buyer) and recommended Zycus, but has not been able to compel IC Hotels to move forward post-demo. If EY's consulting engagement with IC Hotels is on hold, paused, or deprioritized, the Zycus opportunity is blocked. Partner-led motion is stalled, and we have no direct relationship with IC Hotels leadership to bypass the partner.

No Economic Buyer, no Decision Maker, no procurement or operations leadership engaged. CIO alone cannot drive a $200k procurement transformation without CFO/CPO/COO sponsorship. Without executive alignment and budget authority, the deal cannot advance beyond discovery.

Single-thread exposure through CIO Ceyhun Akdemir, who has gone silent. No champion, no multi-threading, no access to procurement or finance stakeholders. If Ceyhun is not the internal advocate or has deprioritized this initiative, we have no alternate path into IC Hotels.

No budget discussion, no ROI quantification, no commercial conversation. Amount $200k is likely a placeholder. Without a validated business case and CFO/CPO sponsorship, budget availability is unknown.

No buyer engagement for 137 days, no follow-up post-demo, no multi-threading, no Economic Buyer mapped, no decision process articulated, no champion behaviors, and no response to the 2026-02-20 materials acknowledgment. The relationship is stalled, and without re-engagement, the deal will remain dormant.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal has been dormant for 137 days since February demo with zero buyer re-engagement, no MEDDPICC coverage, and no forward movement from Qualified stage. Partner-led motion through EY has stalled with no path to close.

best_practice_check.summary: This deal requires immediate re-engagement to determine if it is still alive. 137 days of buyer silence after a single demo is a ghost signal. The highest-leverage actions are (1) restart the conversation via EY (the last active thread) to assess IC Hotels' current priority and timeline, (2) map the Economic Buyer and procurement leadership (CIO alone is insufficient for a $200k procurement transformation), and (3) build a champion or co-champion inside IC Hotels who has urgency and budget access, without which the deal will remain stalled indefinitely.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is currently known or active. Salesforce Competitors__c is null, and the 2026-02-09 demo call contained no reference to other vendors or a competitive shortlist. EY stated they 'evaluated potential solutions and recommended Zycus,' implying an internal EY vendor scan occurred, but the alternatives EY considered were not disclosed. The deal is pre-RFP, pre-shortlist, and has been silent for 137 days, so no active competitive threat is visible. The risk is latent: when (if) IC Hotels or EY formalizes an evaluation, a competitor will likely surface (SAP Ariba as the ERP-native option, Coupa or Jaggaer as enterprise procurement suites, or a regional/hospitality-focused platform). Current threat level is dormant by absence of evidence, not by confirmed sole-source status.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning was present but not emphasized on the 2026-02-09 demo. Products scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake (the AI/agentic layer), and the call title references 'Agentic AI Powered Procurement Solutions,' but the transcript excerpt shows the buyer questions were operationally focused (SAP integration, budget sync, PO workflows) with no questions about AI, negotiation agents, or Merlin capabilities. EY's framing mentioned 'a platform that can shape a solid feature proof process vision,' hinting at future-state/AI potential, but the demo itself appears to have been a traditional procurement-suite walkthrough. With 137 days of silence and no follow-up on AI differentiation, Merlin is under-positioned. The opportunity: hospitality procurement (high-volume, repetitive supplier negotiations, multi-property price variance) is a strong Merlin use case, but that wedge was not activated.

items.quote: Buyer questions 2026-02-09: 'After defining the request properly, can we check the stock position the same with the budget?' and 'Is it possible to instead of SAP, can they download the budget from an external Excel file or something like that, and use it till the SAP budgeting will be active?'

customer_expectations_fit.summary: IC Hotels' articulated needs align well with Zycus' core source-to-pay platform capabilities, particularly around procurement digitalization, ERP integration, and workflow automation. The buyer questions on the 2026-02-09 demo focused on SAP integration (budget sync, PO flows, stock-position checks), external data-source flexibility (budget file uploads as a workaround), and requisition-to-warehouse efficiency (PO-bypass for internal stock). Zycus demoed these workflows, and no gaps or objections were raised on the call. However, fit is unvalidated beyond a single demo: no POC, no use-case workshop, no BRD comparison, no multi-stakeholder review. The 137-day silence suggests either fit concerns emerged post-demo (and were not voiced), or IC Hotels/EY deprioritized the initiative for reasons unrelated to fit (budget, timing, ERP roadmap dependency).
